code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a city (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Identify main subject (first triple subject)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Mapping from predicate to clause template
    phrase_templates = {
        "areaOfWater": "{s} has water area {o}",
        "areaTotal": "{s} has total area {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} is at elevation {o} above sea level",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "populationDensity": "{s} has population density {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} uses area code(s) {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has a {o}",
        "areaOfLand": "{s} has land area {o}",
        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "utcOffset": "{s} has UTC offset {o}",
        "populationTotal": "{s} has total population {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} uses postal code(s) {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s}'s largest city is {o}",
        "language": "{s} uses {o}",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "chairperson": "{s} has chairperson {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} has headquarters at {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s}'s county seat is {o}",
        "capital": "{s}'s capital is {o}",
        "demonym": "people from {s} are called {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{s} includes ethnic group {o}",
        "state": "{s} is in state {o}",
        "populationMetro": "{s} has metropolitan population {o}",
        "type": "{s} is a {o}",
        "governmentType": "{s} has government type {o}",
        "timeZone": "{s} uses time zone {o}",
    }

    # Collect all clauses
    clauses = []
    main_clauses = []
    related_clauses = []

    for triple in triples:
        template = phrase_templates.get(triple.predicate, "{s} {p} {o}")

        # Handle special cases that need processing
        if triple.predicate == "leaderTitle":
            # Convert leader title to lowercase for natural reading
            object_value = triple.object.lower()
        else:
            object_value = triple.object

        clause = template.format(s=triple.subject, p=triple.predicate, o=object_value)

        if triple.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            related_clauses.append(clause)

    # Build single complex sentence - optimized for efficiency and natural flow
    if not main_clauses and not related_clauses:
        return ""

    # Start with main subject's first clause
    if main_clauses:
        sentence_parts = [main_clauses[0].capitalize()]

        # Add remaining main clauses with commas
        for clause in main_clauses[1:]:
            sentence_parts.append(f", {clause}")

        # Add related clauses with appropriate connectors
        for clause in related_clauses:
            clause_lower = clause.lower()
            if "is in" in clause_lower or "is part of" in clause_lower:
                clause_parts = clause.split(' ', 1)
                connector = f", which {clause_parts[1]}" if len(clause_parts) > 1 else f", which {clause}"
                sentence_parts.append(connector)
            elif "capital" in clause_lower or "largest city" in clause_lower:
                if ' ' in clause:
                    connector = f", where the {clause.split(' ', 1)[1]}"
                else:
                    connector = f", where the {clause}"
                sentence_parts.append(connector)
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f", and {clause}")

        return " ".join(sentence_parts) + "."
    else:
        # Fallback: join related clauses for subjects without main subject
        return " ".join(related_clauses).capitalize() + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "utcOffset": "{s} has UTC offset {o}",

        "postalCode": "{s} uses postal code(s) {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s}'s largest city is {o}",
        "language": "{s} uses {o}",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "chairperson": "{s} has chairperson {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} has headquarters at {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s}'s county seat is {o}",
        "capital": "{s}'s capital is {o}",
        "demonym": "people from {s} are called {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{s} includes ethnic group {o}",
        "state": "{s} is in state {o}",
        "populationMetro": "{s} has metropolitan population {o}",
        "type": "{s} is a {o}",
        "governmentType": "{s} has government type {o}",
        "timeZone": "{s} uses time zone {o}",
    }

    # Collect all clauses
    clauses = []
    main_clauses = []
    related_clauses = []

    for triple in triples:
        template = phrase_templates.get(triple.predicate, "{s} {p} {o}")

        # Handle special cases that need processing
        if triple.predicate == "leaderTitle":
            # Convert leader title to lowercase for natural reading
            object_value = triple.object.lower()
        else:
            object_value = triple.object

        clause = template.format(s=triple.subject, p=triple.predicate, o=object_value)

        if triple.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            related_clauses.append(clause)

    # Build single complex sentence
    if main_clauses or related_clauses:
        parts = []

        # Start with main subject's first clause
        if main_clauses:
            parts.append(main_clauses[0].capitalize())

            # Add remaining main clauses
            for clause in main_clauses[1:]:
                parts.append(f", {clause}")

            # Add related clauses with appropriate connectors
            for clause in related_clauses:
                if "is in" in clause or "is part of" in clause:
                    # Extract the predicate part after the first word
                    clause_parts = clause.split(' ', 1)
                    if len(clause_parts) > 1:
                        parts.append(f", which {clause_parts[1]}")
                    else:
                        parts.append(f", which {clause}")
                elif "capital" in clause or "largest city" in clause:
                    parts.append(f", where the {clause.split(' ', 1)[1] if ' ' in clause else clause}")
                else:
                    parts.append(f", and {clause}")
        else:
            # Fallback if no main subject found
            parts.append(" ".join(related_clauses).capitalize())

        sentence = " ".join(parts) + "."
    else:
        sentence = ""

    return sentence
